welcome.subject: Welcome to my newsletter!
welcome.preview: Welcome! A few words about what awaits you...
welcome.tagline: Let’s get started on this little adventure together

content.intro: Thank you for trusting my newsletter. I’m really happy to know that we’ll be able to exchange ideas about design and SaaS.
content.nextSteps: In the coming days, you’ll receive the guide on UX mistakes that I’ve prepared. In the meantime, let me quickly tell you what to expect.
content.frequency: I write to you about once a week, sometimes less if I don't have anything interesting to share. The idea is that each email brings something useful.

whatYouReceive.title: What you will receive:
whatYouReceive.item1: Concrete observations on SaaS product design
whatYouReceive.item3: Useful resources when I come across them

question.title: A quick question to get to know you better:
question.text: What are you working on right now? A SaaS product? A startup? Or are you just interested in interface design?
question.encouragement: Don't hesitate to reply—I like knowing who reads my emails. And if you have any questions or topics you'd like me to cover, let me know.

reminder.title: By the way, a quick note:
reminder.text: If you didn’t sign up for this, no worries. You can simply ignore this email or unsubscribe using the link at the bottom of the page.

newsletterSignup.subject: 🎁 Receive gifts
newsletterSignup.preview: Hi! I wanted to talk to you about something that’s close to my heart...
newsletterSignup.tagline: Problem Solver & Your SaaS Product Designer 🦄

story.intro: A few weeks ago, a user of one of my apps wrote to tell me that it took them a while to undo an action. I realized it was because of a design choice I liked… but that made the experience more complicated.
story.presentation: My name is Adriel, and for 5 years I’ve been designing interfaces for SaaS products, and I’ve noticed something: we often overcomplicate things while users are just looking for simplicity.
story.philosophy: What really interests me is understanding how people use what we create. Why they drop off at a certain point, why they come back, and what truly helps them in their daily lives.

content.title: In this newsletter, I simply share what I’m learning.
content.subtitle: My observations on:
content.item1: What works (and what doesn’t) in the interfaces I create
content.item2: User feedback that changed my mind
content.item3: My mistakes and how I try to avoid them
content.conclusion: Facts, figures, stinging failures, and unexpected victories. Design as it really happens ❤️.

why.title: Why do I really write?
why.reason: Because I like to understand how things work. And in design, there’s often a gap between what we think will work and what actually works.
why.motivation: I feel like sharing these little discoveries, those moments when you think "oh hey, I wouldn’t have thought of that." No pretension, just shared curiosity ❤️.

cta.title: I've prepared something to get started.
cta.description: A guide to the UX mistakes I often notice in SaaS. Concrete observations I’ve gathered throughout my projects.
cta.note: And of course, you can unsubscribe anytime. I’ll only write to you when I have something useful to share.

freeProductDelivery.subject: 🎁 Receive gifts
freeProductDelivery.message1: I hope this resource will be useful for your projects. If you have any questions or feedback after checking it out, don’t hesitate to reply to me directly.
freeProductDelivery.message2: And if you ever use it on a real project, I’d love to hear how it went!

productDelivery.subject: 🎁 Receive gifts
productDelivery.preview: Your {productType} is ready to be downloaded!
productDelivery.message1: I’m glad you’re part of this adventure. I hope this resource helps you go further, faster.
productDelivery.message2: Your feedback really helps me improve my content for the next people. And if you have any questions along the way, don’t hesitate to ask me!

course.feedback: After going through a few modules, I’d really like to get your feedback. Does the content meet your expectations? Are there any areas you’d like to explore in more depth?
course.tip: 💡 Little tip: take notes while you go through the modules—it really helps you remember and apply things.

video.intro: Your masterclass is waiting for you!
video.feedback: Once you've watched the masterclass, tell me what you think! Does it answer your questions? What stood out to you the most?
video.tip: 🎯 Tip: Watch it in a quiet place with a notebook by your side. The best ideas often come while you're listening.

ebook.feedback: When you’ve had time to look through it, I’d like to know what you think. Is it useful for your current projects? What still raises questions for you?
ebook.tip: 📖 Suggestion: read it in small sections and try to apply what you learn right away.
